versioning.compatibility_policy: A consumer repo must record the governance package git SHA it deploys. Release tags improve readability and rollback communication, but tags do not replace the SHA record.

ssot_bootstrap_v2.goal: Make governed repos thin consumers of pinned governance SSOT surfaces while preserving stricter local profile rules.
non_goals: Do not make downstream repos blind mirrors of hldpro-governance.
Do not weaken repo-specific HIPAA, PHI/PII, lane, plan-mode, local runtime, or exemption constraints.
Do not delete local hooks until managed parity is proven.
Do not mutate downstream repos without repo-specific issue-backed PRs.

basis: Downstream e2e proof completed under issue #288 and mirrored by issue #329.
releases.consumer_guidance: Use this tag as the human-readable release coordinate, keep `0.1.0-contract` as the package version for this contract-era release, and keep the exact resolved git SHA in `.hldpro/governance-tooling.json`.

principles: CI remains authoritative; local gates are upstream filters.
Governed repos consume pinned tooling instead of copying shared logic by hand.
Repo-local overrides must be explicit, reviewable, and visible in the consumer record.
Managed files must be identifiable and reversible.
Issue #288 proved downstream pull, deploy, local enforcement, GitHub enforcement, and rollback or uninstall behavior end to end before the first release tag.

surfaces.notes: Runner logic is shared. Consumer repos should not fork this logic.

surfaces.version_rule: pin with runner by governance git SHA
surfaces.notes: Profiles encode repo-specific command mappings while keeping runner behavior shared.

surfaces.version_rule: shim records governance ref supplied at deploy time
surfaces.notes: Managed shims must include the managed marker and must not overwrite unmanaged files without explicit backup or force.
Package-level deployer writes the consumer record and delegates Local CI shim rendering to the compatibility deployer.

surfaces.version_rule: pin by governance git SHA; release tags are human-readable only
surfaces.notes: Consumer repos can prove that their local consumer record and managed files match the governance package they claim to consume. Central GitHub rulesets and repository settings remain report-only from the consumer side and are applied only by issue-backed governance work.

surfaces.version_rule: pin by governance git SHA when consumed outside governance
surfaces.notes: Active parallel roots warn only when declared; undeclared dirty roots still block.

surfaces.version_rule: pin by governance git SHA when used by governed repos
surfaces.notes: Governance-surface changes require issue-backed structured planning. Active issue-lane validation enforces reviewer identity metadata, blocks same-model self-review, requires legal alternate-review exemptions only, and requires review refs once accepted review exists or implementation promotion begins.
Inventory distinguishes local commands, workflow contracts, script dry-runs, and GitHub-only surfaces.

surfaces.version_rule: pin by governance git SHA for helper changes; generated outputs are committed as scoped artifacts
surfaces.notes: Scoped graph/wiki outputs are tracked baselines. Cache and per-run scratch files remain local-only.

paths: CODEX.md tracked session contracts declared in consumer records
repo-local hook adapters declared in consumer records
surfaces.version_rule: pin by governance git SHA; copied downstream hook logic is drift unless declared as a local adapter
surfaces.notes: Hook behavior may be stricter in consumer repos, but stricter local behavior must be declared with owner, reason, and review cadence. Consumer records may represent managed shell hooks, tracked session-contract files such as CLAUDE.md, CODEX.md, docs/EXTERNAL_SERVICES_RUNBOOK.md, and scripts/codex-review.sh, plus hook-settings contracts such as `.claude/settings.json` matcher requirements.

surfaces.version_rule: workflow references must use immutable governance SHAs unless an issue-backed exception exists
verification: consumer verifier reports mutable refs such as @main
surfaces.notes: Reusable workflow refs are package surfaces even when the workflow file itself stays in the consumer repo.

surfaces.version_rule: pin with governance package; local stricter lane policies must be profile extensions
surfaces.notes: HealthcarePlatform strict sandbox/issue-<n>-pr-pending-<scope> lanes are represented as a profile extension, not a global baseline.

paths: repo-local plan-mode hooks declared in profiles
surfaces.version_rule: pin with governance package; repo-local plan gates must be declared
surfaces.notes: Seek plan-mode behavior remains a repo-specific profile extension until generalized.

surfaces.version_rule: pin with governance package after issue #444 lands
verification: handoff package refs once #436/#437 consume events
surfaces.notes: Package v0.2 reserves this surface so called Claude/Codex session evidence can be linked into handoff packages without another bootstrap redesign.

Package consumers should be able to point to lifecycle refs without copying lifecycle validation logic. Lifecycle-state transitions are enforced: implementation-ready and later handoffs must carry the required review/gate refs for their state.

artifact_classes.package_core: Shared logic owned by hldpro-governance. Consumers reference or deploy it from a pinned governance version.
artifact_classes.repo_profile: Repo-specific command mapping owned centrally but selected per consumer.
artifact_classes.managed_file: A file written into a consumer repo by a deployer and marked as managed.
artifact_classes.repo_local_override: Consumer-owned deviation that must be recorded in the consumer manifest and reviewed in that repo.
artifact_classes.profile_extension: A centrally named stricter or specialized policy layer selected by a consumer profile.
artifact_classes.tracked_baseline: Committed measurement or generated artifact used as evidence.
artifact_classes.per_run_report: Local or CI run output that is ignored unless summarized into a closeout or issue comment.

profile_contract.profile_key_policy: Profile keys use governance registry repo slugs and are matched exactly; display names and dashed aliases may be added by verifier/profile mapping code without changing the canonical key.
profile_contract.merge_semantics: Profile inheritance is additive-only by default. A repo-specific extension may add stricter constraints, but it may not remove or weaken constraints inherited from baseline or product-standard layers unless an issue-backed override explicitly allows the narrower behavior and the target profile is not a zero-fail profile.

required_constraints: HIPAA and PHI controls must not be weakened
strict lane naming policy must be preserved
healthcare-specific audit and RLS controls remain authoritative
solo-maintainer or product-baseline overrides where issue-backed

typed_expectations.local_ci_shim: Requires the managed marker and the pinned governance_ref in file content.
typed_expectations.managed_hook: Requires one of the managed markers unless a stricter declared override is recorded.
typed_expectations.tracked_session_contract: Marker-free tracked contract file; may require checksum and required_strings evidence.
typed_expectations.hook_settings_contract: Marker-free `.claude/settings.json` style contract; may require checksum plus required PreToolUse/PostToolUse matcher expectations.

managed_file_contract.overwrite_policy: Refuse unmanaged files by default; require backup or force with issue evidence.
managed_file_contract.rollback_policy: Managed-file rollback must either restore the previous managed version, remove the managed file, or restore an explicit backup. Phase 2 deployer must expose dry-run rollback preview before apply.

forbidden: Forking package core logic without an issue-backed exception.
Disabling CI-required gates through local package configuration.
Treating a dry-run as live enforcement evidence.
Weakening HealthcarePlatform HIPAA, PHI, PII, or strict lane constraints through a baseline override.
Disabling Seek plan-mode enforcement without an issue-backed profile change.
Routing PII away from LAM/local-only constraints through a product profile override.

phase_2_deployer_requires: Support dry-run, apply, verify, and rollback modes.
Write or update the consumer record.
Refuse dirty target repos unless the execution scope explicitly allows the active lane.
Refuse unmanaged file overwrite by default.
phase_gate.phase_4_pilot_fallback_rule: A replacement repo may be selected only if the Phase 4 issue proves local-ai-machine cannot create a clean adoption worktree or cannot run package deployment prerequisites.
